data.title: A wallet suspected to be linked to the whale "dimethyltryptamine.eth" purchased 5.66 million SPIKE tokens.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, five hours ago, the Solana on-chain wallet "fent.sol," suspected to be associated with the whale "dimethyltryptamine.eth," bought 5.66 million SPIKE. This whale previously spent 0.125 ETH to buy 5.9 trillion PEPE and sold it at the peak, getting a return of 52,600 times.

data.title: Ether.fi Restores weETH Across All Chains with Enhanced Security Measures
data.description: Ether.fi announced on the X platform that weETH, based on the LayerZero cross-chain bridge, is now restored across all chains. According to Odaily, the platform has also enabled liquidity minting and redemption features. To enhance security, the ether team has increased the number of Decentralized Verification Networks (DVN) from two to four and implemented stricter rate-limiting mechanisms. Additionally, related services will gradually resume under the guidance of security partners, with more updates to follow.

data.title: Apple's new CEO faces challenges in retaining talent: pressure from executive and engineer attrition is rising.
data.description: Apple's next CEO, John Turners, faces a major challenge as he assumes the role: retaining top talent during a period of historic personnel change. After several relatively calm years, Apple recently experienced a wave of departures, affecting both senior executives and junior engineers. Turners, who will succeed Tim Cook as CEO in September, is tasked with stabilizing the workforce. Late last year, the highly acclaimed chip chief, Johnny Sruggie, told Cook he was seriously considering leaving Apple. Apple recently retained Sruggie by offering him a new compensation package and a broader position—the company's first Chief Hardware Officer. Turners will need to address succession issues and the potential departures of other executives, including Mike Rockwell and several long-time leaders nearing retirement. (Jinshi)

data.title: SpaceX announces it has the option to acquire AI programming startup Cursor for $60 billion.
data.description: SpaceX announced on its X platform that it has reached a cooperation agreement with AI programming startup Cursor, which has granted SpaceX the option to acquire it for $60 billion later this year, or pay $10 billion to advance the collaboration. Cursor is a rapidly growing artificial intelligence startup focused on developing code-writing software.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Advocates for Ethereum's Support of Non-Crypto Developers
data.description: Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin has proposed a model for funding non-crypto developers through Ethereum, emphasizing a dependency-based approach. According to NS3.AI, Buterin highlighted the potential for Ethereum to provide financial support, citing an example of 1.5 ETH in assistance. He also urged open-source projects to explore new cryptoeconomic mechanisms to prevent eventual corporate acquisition.

data.title: Spurs vs. Trail Blazers: High Stakes in NBA Playoff Predictions
data.description:
A significant wager has been placed on the NBA playoff matchup between the San Antonio Spurs and the Portland Trail Blazers. According to Odaily, an account with a 46% win rate has invested $800,000 on the Spurs defeating the Trail Blazers, with an opening price of 84.5¢. Additionally, the account has placed a $50,000 bet on the Spurs winning with a spread of 11.5 points, at an opening price of 50.8¢.

The second game of the Western Conference first-round playoff series began at 8 a.m. UTC+8 today, with the Spurs currently leading the series 1-0. The Spurs' starting lineup includes Vassell, Champagnie, Wembanyama, Fox, and Castle, while the Trail Blazers feature Avdija, Kamara, Clingan, Henderson, and Holiday.

data.title: ether.fi: weETH, based on the LayerZero cross-chain bridge, has resumed operation.
data.description: ether.fi announced on the X platform that weETH, powered by the LayerZero cross-chain bridge, is now back online on all chains, with liquidity minting and redemption functions enabled. Regarding security, the ether team has increased the number of DVNs (Decentralized Validator Networks) from 2 to 4 and implemented a stricter rate limiting mechanism to further enhance system security. Furthermore, related services will be gradually restored under the guidance of security partners, with more updates to be released later.

data.title: Warsh's hearing was high-profile: he denied promising Trump rate cuts, criticized the Fed for being lost, and was called a "puppet."
data.description: Warsh, Trump's handpicked nominee for Federal Reserve Chair, launched a fierce attack during his confirmation hearing: he refused to answer whether Trump had lost the election, prompting Warren to accuse him of being a "puppet"; he countered by criticizing the Fed for being "out of its mind and playing politics"; and he repeatedly denied making any promises to the president regarding low interest rates. Nick Timiraos, often referred to as the "Fed's mouthpiece," wrote that Massachusetts Democratic Senator Elizabeth Warren, in her opening statement, simultaneously labeled Warsh a "puppet" and opportunist for Trump. However, Warren argued that a Fed chair who wouldn't even dare to state simple facts that might displease the president who nominated him wouldn't stand up against that president in crucial moments. This theme permeated the entire hearing, with Democrats repeatedly returning to this point. Warsh also stated that the Fed needs "fundamental policy reforms," ​​including a new inflation framework, new tools, and new ways of communicating. While avoiding Trump's public attacks on the Fed, Warsh repeatedly denied to senators from both parties that Trump had sought any commitments regarding interest rates. "The president has never asked me to pre-determine, commit to, determine, or decide on any interest rate decisions, not in any of our discussions, and I would never agree to do so." (WSJ)

data.title: CICC Research Report: Warsh's statements at the hearing have shown a tendency towards interest rate cuts.
data.description: A research report from CICC states that Federal Reserve Chairman nominee Kevin Warsh, during his hearing before the Senate Banking Committee, revealed his core policy stance of a two-pronged approach: balance sheet reduction and interest rate cuts. At the balance sheet level, he explicitly opposes normalizing quantitative easing (QE), advocating for a gradual and orderly reduction of the Fed's balance sheet size and a withdrawal from fiscal-like responsibilities, returning it to its monetary policy roots. At the interest rate level, although no explicit commitment was made, his statements already indicated a tendency towards rate cuts. Warsh's policy proposals not only represent an adjustment to the monetary issuance mechanism but also an extension of the "America First" strategy under the wave of anti-globalization into the monetary sphere. It shifts from a "global central bank" that endlessly injects liquidity globally to a new approach that firmly controls the overall money supply, focuses on domestic productivity, and emphasizes monetary sovereignty. We believe this shift means the narrative of continued dollar liquidity overload will face revision, and assets solely reliant on liquidity and benefiting from "dollar over-issuance" may come under pressure. (Jinshi)

data.title: Coinbase will list Diem (DIEM)
data.description: Coinbase has announced that it will list Diem (DIEM). The DIEM-USD trading pair will be available on or after 9:00 AM Pacific Time on April 22, 2026, provided that liquidity conditions are met and the region supports trading.

data.title: OpenAI announces the release of ChatGPT Images 2.0 image model
data.description: OpenAI has announced the release of ChatGPT Images 2.0, a new image model that significantly enhances the processing capabilities of complex visual tasks, with upgrades in instruction understanding, object placement and relationship representation, and high-density text rendering. This model supports multilingual text generation, accurately presents non-English content in images, and improves overall semantic coherence. ChatGPT Images 2.0 is available to all ChatGPT and Codex users, with the "thinking" image functionality available to Plus, Pro, and Business users (Enterprise support coming soon). The underlying model, gpt-image-2, is also available via API.

data.title: X Introduces Custom Timelines Feature for Enhanced User Experience
data.description:
On April 22, X announced the launch of its 'Custom Timelines' feature, aimed at enhancing user engagement by allowing specific topics to be pinned on the homepage tab. According to BlockBeats, this new feature supports over 75 interest topics, enabling users to explore niche content areas more deeply.

The feature is powered by Grok's content understanding and platform algorithms, offering personalized recommendations that dynamically generate timelines based on user interests. This is particularly effective for topics where users already have interaction preferences. Currently, early access to this feature is available for Premium subscribers on the iOS platform, with an Android version expected to be released soon.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Apple's Slow Progress in AI Leaves Company Dependent on Competitors
data.description: Apple is facing challenges in the artificial intelligence sector, with its progress described as slow and its organizational structure fragmented. According to PANews, the company currently relies on competitors like Google, highlighting a passive stance in the AI domain. This situation has been referred to as a significant 'AI debt' left by CEO Tim Cook, indicating the need for strategic advancements in AI to maintain competitiveness.

data.title: Digital bank Revolut's IPO valuation could reach as high as $200 billion.
data.description: According to sources, UK digital bank Revolut plans to seek a valuation of $150 billion to $200 billion in its future IPO, a significant increase from its previous valuation of $75 billion. CEO Nik Storonsky also revealed that Revolut is preparing for a new round of secondary share sales in the second half of 2026, with a valuation potentially exceeding $100 billion. (Financial Times) Previously, Storonsky stated that the company's listing would take at least two more years, possibly delayed until 2028, with no IPO plans in the short term. He pointed out that Revolut is currently focusing on expanding in the US market, including applying for a banking license to gain access to Federal Reserve payment channels and expand its lending and credit card businesses. Meanwhile, the company has already obtained a full banking license in the UK.

data.title: The probability of the Federal Reserve keeping interest rates unchanged in April is 100%.
data.description: According to CME's "FedWatch": the probability of the Federal Reserve raising interest rates by 25 basis points in April is 0%, and the probability of keeping interest rates unchanged is 100%. The probability of the Federal Reserve cutting interest rates by a cumulative 25 basis points by June is 2.5%, and the probability of keeping interest rates unchanged is 97.5%.

data.title: Trump: The new agreement will be much better than the Iran nuclear deal; the ceasefire is "extremely unlikely" to be extended.
data.description: In a tweet, Trump stated that the agreement we are negotiating with Iran will be far better than the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (JCPOA) (the Iran nuclear deal). The latter, drafted by Barack Hussein Obama and Sleepy Joe Biden, is one of the worst security agreements in the history of our country. It was a guaranteed path to nuclear weapons—something the agreement we are negotiating will not and cannot allow. They (the previous administration) loaded $1.7 billion in cash onto Boeing 757s and sent it to Iran, allowing the Iranian leadership to spend it as they pleased. They emptied banks in Washington, Virginia, and Maryland. Those bankers said they had never seen anything like it. In addition, hundreds of billions of dollars were paid to Iran. If I hadn't terminated that "agreement," nuclear weapons would have been used to attack Israel and various parts of the Middle East, including our cherished U.S. military bases. An agreement reached during Trump's term will guarantee peace, security, and safeguards, not only for Israel and the Middle East, but also for Europe, the United States, and everywhere else. This will be something the whole world can be proud of, instead of the years of embarrassment and humiliation we have been forced to endure due to incompetent and cowardly leadership, as we have done for many years. (Jin Shi)

data.title: US senators pushed for a delay in the review of the crypto market structure bill until May, in order to allow more time for negotiations on stablecoin solutions.
data.description: U.S. Senator Thom Tillis is urging the Senate Banking Committee to postpone its consideration of the crypto market structure bill until May to allow more time for banks and the crypto industry to finalize a compromise on stablecoin regulation. This move aims to provide additional room for negotiation on key disagreements surrounding stablecoins and to push for a more viable regulatory framework. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: SpaceX has agreed to acquire Cursor for more than $50 billion.
data.description: SpaceX has agreed to acquire Cursor, a rapidly growing artificial intelligence startup focused on developing code-writing software, for more than $50 billion, according to two sources familiar with the matter. SpaceX is making this deal as it prepares for what could be one of the largest IPOs in history. Founded in 2022, Cursor has raised more than $3 billion and has made headlines as one of the fastest-growing AI startups. SpaceX previously stated that Cursor had granted SpaceX an option to acquire it for $60 billion, or SpaceX could choose to pay $10 billion for the fruits of their collaboration. (Reuters)

data.title: Iran Agrees to Cease Military Combat Amid Ongoing Tensions
data.description: Iran has agreed to halt military combat, according to Iranian state television. However, the broadcaster emphasized that the conflict is not yet resolved. According to NS3.AI, control over the strategic Strait of Hormuz remains a crucial element in the ongoing tensions. The state TV also highlighted that any negotiations must ensure the preservation of Iran's independence and dignity, as well as safeguard its defense, missile, and nuclear capabilities.

data.title: AI TRENDS | New Energy Vehicle Exports Surpass Expectations Amid Geopolitical Tensions
data.description: According to Jin10, a recent report by Xinjian Investment Securities highlights that the China Passenger Car Association's March data release this week showed that wholesale and export figures exceeded expectations. Amid high oil prices due to overseas geopolitical conflicts, the export of new energy passenger vehicles has become a structurally strong factor. The report suggests actively seizing opportunities in high-growth sectors, noting that AI-driven advancements in fuel engine efficiency continue to surpass expectations. Additionally, Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology has been approved in the Netherlands, and the high growth in the robotics sector is driving ongoing industry evolution. Currently, the stock prices of leading companies in these sectors have stabilized, and with an upcoming auto show, the report recommends actively positioning investments in anticipation of new catalysts in the sector.
